title: Voidraker

The '''''Voidraker''''' was the personal [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] of the notorious [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]] during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]]. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="marvel28" /> one of Jabba's [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenants]], the [[Nimbanel/Legends|Nimbanel]] accountant [[Mosep Binneed/Legends|Mosep Binneed]]—who occasionally traveled and worked under Jabba's name—<ref name="redfive" /> flew the ''Voidraker'' to the desolate [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Orleon]], where he had tracked a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] named [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] who owed his master a sizable debt. Binneed and the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] crew cornered Solo's [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] in a cave system, only for Solo's ship to escape by blasting through the cavern's roof. While this was occurring, the ''Voidraker'' was set upon by a massive swarm of [[Stone mite/Legends|stone mites]], which threatened to chew through the entire ship. The stone mite infestation rendered the ''Voidraker'' unusable and killed the entire crew except for Binneed, who made his way to safety thanks to a lift from Solo.<ref name="marvel28" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Voidraker'' first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 28|''Star Wars'' (1977) 28]], written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and released in [[1979]]. The ship was illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]].<ref name="marvel28" />
